pois.name: Schloss Neuschwanstein
pois.description: Commissioned by Ludwig II in the 19th century, this Romanesque Revival palace is one of the most famous landmarks in Germany and inspired the Disney castle.

pois.name: Würzburger Residenz
pois.description: A masterpiece of German Baroque architecture and former residence of the Würzburg Prince-Bishops, featuring a massive staircase with a ceiling fresco by Tiepolo.

pois.name: Steinerne Brücke Regensburg
pois.description: Completed in 1146, this is Germany's oldest preserved stone bridge and was a major crossing point for the Danube for centuries.

pois.name: Walhalla
pois.description: A neoclassical hall of fame honoring distinguished Germans, built in the form of a Parthenon-style temple overlooking the Danube.

pois.name: Bamberger Dom
pois.description: A late Romanesque imperial cathedral known for the 'Bamberg Horseman' sculpture and the only papal grave north of the Alps.

pois.name: Kaiserburg Nürnberg
pois.description: A historic fortification that was a residence for every Holy Roman Emperor between 1050 and 1571, representing Nuremberg's importance in the Middle Ages.

pois.name: Wieskirche
pois.description: A stunning Rococo pilgrimage church in the Alpine foothills, famous for its elaborate stucco work and vibrant ceiling frescoes.

pois.name: Kloster Weltenburg
pois.description: Located at the Danube Gorge, this Benedictine abbey features a spectacular Asam brothers church and one of the oldest monastery breweries in the world.

pois.name: Altstadt Rothenburg ob der Tauber
pois.description: A remarkably well-preserved medieval town surrounded by intact defensive walls, towers, and half-timbered houses.

pois.name: Schloss Herrenchiemsee
pois.description: Built by King Ludwig II on an island in the Chiemsee lake, this palace was intended as a replica of Versailles but remained unfinished.

pois.name: Passauer Dom St. Stephan
pois.description: A Baroque cathedral containing the world's largest cathedral organ with 17,974 pipes, situated at the confluence of three rivers.

pois.name: Schloss Linderhof
pois.description: The smallest of the three palaces built by Ludwig II and the only one he lived to see completed, featuring an elaborate Venus Grotto.

pois.name: Marienplatz München
pois.description: The central square of Munich since 1158, dominated by the New Town Hall and its famous glockenspiel.

pois.name: Frauenkirche München
pois.description: A Gothic cathedral and symbol of the Bavarian capital, recognizable by its twin onion-domed towers and the 'Devil's Footstep' at the entrance.

pois.name: Burg zu Burghausen
pois.description: At over 1,000 meters in length, this is the longest castle complex in the world, consisting of six courtyards and numerous defensive structures.

pois.name: Partnachklamm
pois.description: A 700-meter long gorge in Garmisch-Partenkirchen with 80-meter high limestone walls and dramatic cascading water.

pois.name: Zugspitze
pois.description: The highest peak in Germany at 2,962 meters, offering panoramic views of the Alps across four different countries.

pois.name: Königssee
pois.description: A deep, fjord-like emerald lake in the Berchtesgaden National Park, famous for its silent electric boats and Echo Wall.

pois.name: Kloster Andechs
pois.description: A Benedictine monastery and pilgrimage site known as the 'Holy Mountain' of Bavaria, famous for its traditional monastic brewery.

pois.name: Befreiungshalle Kelheim
pois.description: A massive neoclassical rotunda commissioned by King Ludwig I to commemorate the victory over Napoleon in the Wars of Liberation.

pois.name: Fuggerei
pois.description: The world's oldest social housing complex still in use, founded by Jakob Fugger in 1516 where the rent is still 0.88 euros per year.

pois.name: Basilika Vierzehnheiligen
pois.description: A late Baroque-Rococo basilica designed by Balthasar Neumann, dedicated to the Fourteen Holy Helpers.

pois.name: Schloss Johannisburg
pois.description: A massive red sandstone Renaissance palace in Aschaffenburg that served as a secondary residence for the Archbishops of Mainz.

pois.name: Kloster Ottobeuren
pois.description: One of the most important Benedictine abbeys in Germany, known for its monumental Baroque basilica and library.

pois.name: Lindau Hafeneinfahrt
pois.description: The harbor entrance of Lindau on Lake Constance, featuring the Bavarian Lion sculpture and the southernmost lighthouse in Germany.

pois.name: Limes-Thermen Weißenburg
pois.description: The remains of a Roman military bath complex located near the Raetian Limes, showcasing advanced ancient heating and plumbing.

pois.name: Luisenburg-Felsenlabyrinth
pois.description: A natural block field of granite boulders in the Fichtelgebirge, forming a unique labyrinthine hiking area.

pois.name: Burgruine Wolfstein
pois.description: The ruins of a high medieval hilltop castle overlooking Neumarkt, providing insights into regional fortifications of the 12th century.

pois.name: Marktplatz Miltenberg
pois.description: A picturesque market square featuring the 'Schnatterloch' and several historic half-timbered houses often called the 'Bavarian Rothenburg'.

pois.name: Schloss Seehof
pois.description: The former summer residence and hunting lodge of the Bamberg Prince-Bishops, featuring extensive gardens and a large water basin.

pois.name: Veste Coburg
pois.description: Known as the 'Franconian Crown', this is one of the largest castle complexes in Germany and once housed Martin Luther.

pois.name: Donaudurchbruch
pois.description: A narrow section of the Danube valley near Kelheim where the river has cut through massive limestone rocks.

pois.name: KZ-Gedenkstätte Dachau
pois.description: The site of the first Nazi concentration camp, preserved as a memorial and museum to honor the victims and educate visitors.

pois.name: Kloster Ettal
pois.description: A Benedictine abbey in the Ammergau Alps known for its massive domed church and traditional production of liqueur and beer.

pois.name: Residenz Ansbach
pois.description: A former palace of the Margraves of Brandenburg-Ansbach, featuring early 18th-century state rooms and a fine collection of porcelain.

pois.name: Nördlingen Stadtmauer
pois.description: The only town wall in Germany that can be walked all the way around, surrounding a town built inside a meteorite crater.

pois.name: Kloster Banz
pois.description: A former Benedictine abbey overlooking the Main valley, now used as an educational center, known for its Baroque architecture.

pois.name: Burg Trausnitz
pois.description: The ancestral seat of the Wittelsbach dynasty in Landshut, overlooking the city with its medieval fortifications and Renaissance interior.

pois.name: Scheidegger Wasserfälle
pois.description: A series of impressive waterfalls near Lake Constance where the Rickenbach river drops over several rocky ledges.

pois.name: Stiftsbibliothek Waldsassen
pois.description: A magnificent Cistercian abbey library featuring ten life-sized wood carvings that symbolize the different facets of human vanity.

pois.name: Burg Prunn
pois.description: A 12th-century castle perched on a sheer limestone cliff, where a famous manuscript of the Nibelungenlied was discovered.

pois.name: Drachenstich-Arena
pois.description: The site of Germany's oldest folk play in Furth im Wald, featuring a massive high-tech robotic dragon.

pois.name: Hofbräuhaus am Platzl
pois.description: Munich's most famous beer hall, originally founded as a royal brewery for the Duke of Bavaria.

pois.name: Kuchlbauer-Turm
pois.description: A unique 35-meter tall tower designed by Friedensreich Hundertwasser, located at the Kuchlbauer Brewery in Abensberg.

pois.name: Teufelshöhle Pottenstein
pois.description: The largest stalactite cave in Franconian Switzerland, featuring impressive natural formations and cave bear skeletons.

pois.name: Maria Gern
pois.description: A small Baroque pilgrimage church offering one of the most iconic photographic views of the Watzmann massif.

pois.name: Plassenburg
pois.description: A monumental Hohenzollern fortress in Kulmbach, housing the world's largest museum of tin soldiers.

pois.name: Kloster Plankstetten
pois.description: A Benedictine abbey in the Altmühltal Nature Park known for its commitment to ecological farming and traditional monastic crafts.

pois.name: Gasthaus zum Riesen
pois.description: One of the oldest inns in Germany, located in Miltenberg, which has hosted several emperors and famous historical figures.

pois.name: Eibsee
pois.description: A crystal-clear alpine lake at the foot of the Zugspitze, created by a massive rockfall thousands of years ago.

pois.name: Wendelstein
pois.description: A prominent peak in the Mangfall Mountains accessible by the oldest rack railway in Bavaria, offering views across the Alps.

pois.name: Eichstätter Dom
pois.description: A cathedral blending Romanesque, Gothic, and Baroque styles, containing the monumental Pappenheim Altar.

pois.name: Kehlsteinhaus
pois.description: Also known as the Eagle's Nest, this Third Reich-era building sits atop a mountain ridge and is now a restaurant with somber historical significance.

pois.name: Steinerne Rinne Wolfsbronn
pois.description: A rare natural limestone channel formed by calcifying spring water flowing down a forest slope.

pois.name: Burg Pottenstein
pois.description: The oldest castle in Franconian Switzerland, serving as a landmark on a rocky outcrop above the town.

pois.name: Altes Rathaus Bamberg
pois.description: An iconic town hall built on an artificial island in the river Regnitz, featuring ornate exterior frescoes.

pois.name: Volkacher Mainschleife
pois.description: The largest river bend of the Main, offering scenic views over Franconian vineyards and the pilgrimage church Maria im Weingarten.

pois.name: Historische Brauereigaststätte Schlenkerla
pois.description: A famous 15th-century tavern in Bamberg known for serving traditional Aecht Schlenkerla Rauchbier (smoked beer).

pois.name: Stadtresidenz Landshut
pois.description: The first Renaissance palace built north of the Alps, modeled after the Palazzo Te in Mantua, Italy.

pois.name: Gnadenkapelle Altötting
pois.description: The 'Lourdes of Germany', this octagonal chapel houses a 14th-century Black Madonna and the hearts of many Bavarian kings.
